agentDetail.configure.advancedSettings.d: For power users. Env vars, sandbox & memory.

If your .env file is hidden, enable hidden files in your system file picker.
To show hidden .env files, enable Hidden items in File Explorer.

Set values the agent reads at runtime, like an API key a tool needs, without putting them in the Prompt.

In Build mode, the agent can set these up for you.

Set values the agent reads at runtime, like an API key a tool needs, without putting them in the Prompt.

In Build mode, the agent can set these up for you.
agentDetail.configure.build.empty.commun: Community Edition, while offering versioning to the extracted configs, does not support versioning on the file system itself. Be careful with your actions in Build mode, as changes made to the file system happen in real time and cannot always be neatly reverted. Community Edition is not your ideal choice for serving mass external audiences.
agentDetail.configure.build.empty.descri: Describe what you want and it fills in the form on the left as you go.
agentDetail.configure.build.empty.title: Build your agent by chatting
agentDetail.configure.build.inputPlaceho: Describe what your agent should do
agentDetail.configure.buildDraft.buildNo: A running note of this build the agent uses to keep context across sessions. It's added to your files when you Apply.
agentDetail.configure.buildDraft.modeDes: You’re in build mode. Configure can only be updated by the agent in this mode. Shape this setup through the chat on the right, then Apply.
agentDetail.configure.buildDraft.title: Build draft
agentDetail.configure.chatFeatures.descr: Shape the end-user chat experience on your web app and chat surfaces.
agentDetail.configure.chatFeatures.title: Chat Features
agentDetail.configure.clearSessionConfir: This will clear the current session and discard unapplied Agent configuration changes.
agentDetail.configure.communityEditionIs: Community Edition does not provide hard file system isolation between end users or runs. Do not expose the same CE agent to multiple independent end users where data isolation or strict compliance is required.
agentDetail.configure.files.buildNote.ri: The agent's record of what it set up in Build mode. It reads this at the start of every conversation, alongside your Prompt. <docLink>Learn more</docLink>
agentDetail.configure.files.buildNote.to: The agent's record of what it set up in Build mode. It reads this at the start of every conversation, alongside your Prompt. Learn more

agentDetail.configure.files.label: Files
agentDetail.configure.files.preview.unsu: Preview is not supported for this file.
agentDetail.configure.files.tip:
For reference material too long to paste into the Prompt: specs, templates, guidelines. Reference with / in the Prompt.

agentDetail.configure.knowledgeRetrieval: Click the “+” button to add knowledge
The agent writes its own query from the prompt and available context. Nothing to set up here.
Write a fixed query for this retrieval source.

agentDetail.configure.model.label: Model
agentDetail.configure.preview.empty.desc: Run the agent as a finished chat, exactly how people will experience it once published.
agentDetail.configure.preview.empty.noIn: No instructions yet, so replies come from the plain model.
agentDetail.configure.preview.sandboxNot: Agent runs in a Linux sandbox.
For Dify Community Edition, each of your agents runs in a Linux 7.0.0-10060-aws sandbox environment within your docker. Your edits to the environment via Build Chats are persistent.
agentDetail.configure.preview.title: Preview
agentDetail.configure.prompt.label: Prompt
agentDetail.configure.prompt.tip: Define the agent's role and describe its typical task. Use / to make explicit reference to Skills, Files, Tools and Knowledge Retrievals.
agentDetail.configure.rightPanel.buildTi: Build sets up the agent by chatting. Describe what you want and it fills in the setup on the left.
agentDetail.configure.rightPanel.preview: Preview is available on Dify Cloud and Enterprise only.
Preview runs the finished agent the way your users will see it, with clean replies and chat features.
agentDetail.configure.skills.empty.descr: Skills give the agent reusable expertise it can call while working
agentDetail.configure.skills.empty.title: No skills yet
agentDetail.configure.skills.label: Skills
agentDetail.configure.skills.richTip:

agentDetail.configure.skills.upload.drop: Drop a skill package here, or
agentDetail.configure.skills.upload.warn: If you only need to use Markdown files, upload them to Files and reference them in your prompt.
Uploaded skills must follow the <specificationLink>Agent Skills specification</specificationLink>.
agentDetail.configure.title: Configure
agentDetail.configure.tools.addMenu.cliT: Install a command line program the agent runs in its sandbox. Needs install and usually API keys.
agentDetail.configure.tools.addMenu.tool: Pick a tool already in this workspace, like web search or integrations.
agentDetail.configure.tools.cliDialog.de: Install a CLI tool from any package registry. The agent gains shell access to the binary it provides.
agentDetail.configure.tools.cliDialog.en: Store API keys, tokens, or secrets that this CLI tool needs. Values are encrypted at rest and injected as environment variables when the agent invokes the tool.
agentDetail.configure.tools.cliDialog.in: Sandboxed · runs once on first invocation
Paste your install command here, e.g. npx -y @playwright/mcp@latest  ·  uv tool install httpie
agentDetail.configure.tools.cliDialog.se: Never visible to agent prompt; injected only as $VAR at runtime
agentDetail.configure.tools.empty.descri: Tools let the agent act, like searching the web or calling your apps
agentDetail.configure.tools.empty.title: No tools yet
agentDetail.configure.tools.label: Tools
agentDetail.configure.tools.richTip:
Add Dify tools from your workspace. Reference with / in the Prompt. <docLink>Learn more</docLink>

Tools the agent installs in its sandbox won't appear here but stay available to it.

agentDetail.monitoring.metrics.totalConv: Daily AI conversations that reached this agent through the selected access point.
agentDetail.monitoring.metrics.userSatis: The number of likes per 1,000 messages for this agent.
